gill: noun. A breathing organ of fish and other aquatic animals.

gilt: noun. Gold or other metal in a thin layer; gilding.

hill: noun. An elevated landmass smaller than a mountain.
hilly: adjective. Abundant in hills; having many hills.

hist: interjection. An utterance used to discreetly attract someone's attention.
hit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hittite.

its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
light: noun. Electromagnetic radiation in the wavelength range visible to the human eye (about 400–750 nanometers): visible light.
lilt: verb. To do something rhythmically, with animation and quickness, usually of music.
lily: noun. Any of several flowers in the genus Lilium of the family Liliaceae, which includes a great many ornamental species.

list: noun. A strip of fabric, especially from the edge of a piece of cloth.

shit: noun. Usually solid excretory product evacuated from the bowels; feces.

sightly: adjective. Attractive, pleasing to the eye; affording gratification to the sense of sight; aesthetically pleasing.
sill: noun. A breast wall; window breast; horizontal brink which forms the base of a window.
silly: adjective. Laughable or amusing through foolishness or a foolish appearance.
silt: noun. Mud or fine earth deposited from running or standing water.
silty: adjective. Having a noticeable amount of silt.
